raw_user_request: Build a client intake form for a tax prep office.
concrete_goal: Build a browser-based client intake form for tax preparers who need to collect filing status, document checklist progress, appointment readiness, and follow-up tasks from the first screen.
success_criteria: Tax preparers can add, edit, validate, and filter client intake records without leaving the first screen.
The interface exposes filing status, document checklist, appointment readiness, follow-up task, empty state, loading state, validation state, and saved-success state.
scope_in: Single-page tax client intake UI with local client-side state.
Seeded fictional client records, checklist items, readiness statuses, and responsive controls.
scope_out: Real tax filing, e-signature, payments, authentication, document upload storage, and production deployment.
assumptions: The prototype uses fictional client records and does not collect real tax documents.
Local browser state is acceptable for the first usable version.
implementation_constraints: Do not add paid APIs, real tax advice, or production storage.
Keep the primary intake workflow available on the first screen.

target_users: Tax preparers and office intake coordinators who track fictional clients, required filing documents, appointment readiness, and follow-up tasks.
product_brief.core_problem: Tax office intake work is easy to lose across emails and spreadsheets when preparers cannot see missing documents, filing status, and appointment readiness together.
primary_workflows: Create or update a fictional client intake record with filing status, document checklist, appointment date, owner, readiness status, and next follow-up task.
Filter clients by missing documents, urgent follow-up, and appointment readiness so preparers can prioritize intake work.
Review empty, loading, validation, disabled, saved-success, and recoverable error states on desktop and mobile.
domain_assumptions: Use tax office intake vocabulary such as filing status, W-2 checklist, appointment readiness, preparer owner, follow-up task, and missing document.
content_data_model_assumptions: Primary entity: intake record with fields id, fictional client name, filing status, preparer owner, appointment date, readiness status, checklist items, follow-up task, notes, updated time, and validation state.

Checklist items include label, required flag, received flag, and status values missing, received, or waived; UI states include first-run empty queue, loading seed records, save success, validation error for missing filing status, disabled save while invalid, and recoverable local state error.
non_goals: No real taxpayer information, tax advice, document upload storage, payment processing, authentication, or production deployment.

scenario_flows.name: Create tax client intake
preconditions: The intake queue is loaded with fictional seed records.
steps: Open the add client form from the first screen.
Enter fictional client name, filing status, preparer owner, appointment date, checklist items, and follow-up task.
Save the record and confirm it appears in the missing-documents queue.
scenario_flows.expected_result: The saved intake record appears with readiness status, checklist count, and next follow-up task.
failure_or_edge_cases: Missing filing status shows validation and disables save.
A simulated save error preserves draft values and shows recovery guidance.

scenario_flows.name: Filter readiness queue
preconditions: At least one fictional client has missing documents and one is ready.
steps: Filter by missing W-2 checklist items.
Mark one checklist item received and clear the filter.
scenario_flows.expected_result: Readiness counts, client visibility, and checklist status update without hiding urgent follow-up tasks.
failure_or_edge_cases: Empty filter results show a reset control.
Long follow-up notes wrap on mobile without covering controls.

verification_matrix.build: Run the repository build command when present or document why no build command exists.
verification_matrix.lint: Run lint or equivalent static checks when present.
verification_matrix.run: Start the local app or open the static artifact and report the URL or file path.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Exercise create, filter, validation, empty, error, desktop, mobile, and keyboard/focus behavior.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Desktop 1440x900 screenshot or visual inspection artifact showing the tax intake queue, checklist filters, form, and detail area with no overlap.
Mobile 390x844 screenshot or visual inspection artifact showing stacked intake records, reachable save controls, and wrapped follow-up notes.
Behavior evidence for creating a client intake record, filtering missing documents, marking a checklist item received, and observing updated readiness counts.
Accessibility evidence covering labels, visible focus, keyboard operation, and contrast-sensitive readiness indicators.
State coverage evidence for empty, loading, error, saved-success, disabled, and validation states.

iteration_policy.threshold_rule: overall_completion_score must be at least quality_score_threshold.
iteration_policy.rework_rule: Any failed app-quality evidence creates concrete rework before acceptance.
stop_conditions: Review is accepted with all app evidence present.
A real taxpayer data or production storage blocker is discovered.

delegation_plan_seed.objective: Implement the first-screen tax client intake workflow.
